entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: An invasive aquatic macrophyte species, also known as Pontederia crassipes or Eichhornia crassipes, indigenous to South America.

entities.name: AMAZONIA
entities.description: A region in Brazil where water hyacinth is indigenous.

entities.name: BRAZIL
entities.description: A country in South America where water hyacinth is indigenous.

entities.name: SOUTH AMERICA
entities.description: A continent where water hyacinth is indigenous.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where water hyacinth was introduced in 1896 and has become an environmental challenge.

entities.name: VEMBANAD LAKE
entities.description: A lake in Kuttanad, India, used as the focus area for detecting water hyacinth.

entities.name: KUTTANAD
entities.description: A region in India where Vembanad Lake is located.

entities.name: SATELLITE IMAGING
entities.description: A remote sensing practice used to provide synoptic coverage for monitoring aquatic plants.

entities.name: REMOTE SENSING
entities.description: The practice of detecting aquatic plants from a distance, often using satellite data.

entities.name: SYNTHETIC APERTURE RADAR
entities.description: A type of radar (SAR) used for the detection of water hyacinth.

entities.name: SENTINEL-1 SAR
entities.description: A specific dual-polarization SAR imagery source used to monitor water hyacinth.

entities.name: CHANGE DETECTORS
entities.description: Traditional and state-of-the-art tools used for quantitative analysis of detection performance.

entities.name: INFESTATION
entities.description: The state of water hyacinth spreading and causing adverse impacts in waterbodies.

entities.name: INVASIVENESS
entities.description: The characteristic of water hyacinth to propagate rapidly under conducive conditions.
